title: Zambia%20Environmental%20Management%20Agency%20Case%20Study
content:
/CASE STUD Y
CONTINUOUS ENVIRONMENTAL 
MANAGEMENT SYSTEM (CEMS)Zambia Environmental Management 
Agency (ZEMA) automated the registration 
and checking of businesses compliance with 
environmental regulations through and 
online portal

Zambia Environmental Management Agency (ZEMA) is an independent 
environmental regulator and coordinating agency, established through an 
Act of Parliament, the Environmental Management Act no 12 of 2011. It is 
mandated to do all such things as are necessary to protect the environ-
ment and control pollution, so as to provide for the health and welfare of 
persons, animals, plants and the environment. ZEMA is governed by a 
board which provides strategic direction, develops policies approves its 
work plans and budget as well as monitoring of its functions as it relates to 
administration of the EMA. 
The mandate of ZEMA is to ensure sustainable management of natural 
resources, protection of the environment and prevention and control of 
pollution. Individual functions of ZEMA are as follows: 
1. Advising government and the private sector on environmental 
management and pollution control matters. 
2. Monitoring trends of natural resource usage and resulting impacts 
on the environment 
3. Advising the Minister on declarations of protected areas and their 
management. 
4. Gathering and disseminating information to the public on environ-
mental protection and pollution control 
5. Initiating and promoting research, training & investigations in 
environmental management 
6. Coordinating the implementation of activities of ministries & other 
authorities in environmental management 
7. Conservation of biodiversity 
8. Licensing undertakings and processes involving waste water, 
hazardous waste, Ozone Depleting Substances and chemicals 
9. Ensuring the integration of environmental concerns in national 
planning 
10. Environmental management through tools such as SoE, NEAP, 
Environmental Management Strategies, EIA & SEA. 
11. Environmental protection and pollution control. 
ZEMA was managing all business processes and licensing 
applications through a manual process. Also there were no 
automated system to check compliance with various regulatory 
requirements related to Environmental management. 
Techno Brain designed, developed and implemented an e-ZEMA 
Portal to automate various business processes within ZEMA 
especially those processes which allows companies within ZAMBIA 
to apply and obtain various licenses and permits.Cust omer:

Solution:
 
 
 Quick Shee t
Business Situa tion
Solution
Continuous Environmental Management System 
(CEMS)
ZEMA was managing all business processes and licensing 
applications through a manual process. Also there were no 
automated system to check compliance with various 
regulatory requirements related to Environmental 
management. 
Techno Brain designed, developed and implemented an 
e-ZEMA Portal to automate various business processes within 
ZEMA especially those processes which allows companies 
within ZAMBIA to apply and obtain various licenses and 
permits.
• Improved eﬃciency in service delivery with the 
elimination of bottlenecks faced through manual 
processes. 
• Online interaction with clients through eZEMA portal.  
• Reduced costs and increased revenue collection for the 
agency through eﬀective management of environmental 
permits.  
• Improved eﬃciency in monitoring of environmental 
permits and statutory returns.  
• Improved environmental compliance by industry, 
resulting in positive impact on the overall environmental 
management in the country. 
• Improved eﬃciency and integrity of ZEMA through 
segregation of duties and audit trails. 
• Improved speed of response by ZEMA in carrying out 
environmental impact assessment process and response 
to reported issues.

 
 Major Features of the Solution 
ProvidedTechnologies
a. Clients Module – Allowing Online Services through the Web 
Portal
b. Licensing Module
i. Management of all licenses - Emissions (eﬄuent, land and 
air), Waste Management, Hazardous Waste, Pesticides and 
Toxic substances, and Ozone Depleting Substances.
c. Inspection /Compliance Monitoring module
i. Inspection of Licensee site
ii. Inspection status
iii. Inspection output Reports Management
d. d. Statutory Returns:
i. Management of all returns submitted by Licensee
ii. Licensee can view submitted and due Returns
e. e. Environmental Impact Assessment Register
i. Environmental Impact Statements upload
ii. Environmental Project Briefs upload
iii. Strategic Environmental Assessments upload
iv. Environment Assessments Update
f. f. Environmental Complaints
g. g. Integration Map Server
h. h. Reports
i. Report of Licensing Module
ii. Reports of Inspection
iii. Reports of Returns Module
iv. Reports of EIA Module
v. Reports for Legal Department
vi. Reports for Accounts Department
vii. Reports of Complaints and Environment Emergency
viii. Reports for AuditMySQL

• Improved eﬃciency in monitoring of environmental permits and 
statutory returns.  
• Improved environmental compliance by industry, resulting in

• Improved eﬃciency and integrity of ZEMA through segregation 
of duties and audit trails. 
• Improved speed of response by ZEMA in carrying out

reported issues.
